entries.intro: The scales at the market always seem to lean one way. Funny how that works.
details: I bought grain there twice last month and came up short both times. Weighed it myself at home.
When I mentioned it, the seller just shrugged and said his weights were certified. Sure they are.
reactions: I'm not making a scene over a few handfuls of grain. But I notice.

statCheck.pass: A thumb on the pan or a shaved weight: it's the oldest trick there is. Hard to prove unless you bring your own scale.
statCheck.fail: Maybe I miscounted. But it's happened more than once.

entries.intro: I heard someone on the local council has been doing favors for friends. Surprise, surprise.
details: A contract went to a fellow who's not exactly the most qualified, if you know what I mean. He's just well-connected.
Nobody said anything publicly: they never do. But people noticed.
reactions: It's not exactly shocking. People help their friends. Doesn't make it right.

entries.intro: A merchant in town got caught watering down his stock. Took people long enough to figure it out.
details: He'd been doing it for months, apparently. Just a little at a time so nobody would taste the difference.
Someone finally compared a fresh bottle to an old one side by side: night and day.
reactions: At least he got caught. Most of the time people just grumble and keep buying.

entries.intro: Taxes went up again and nobody can tell me where the money goes. Funny, that.
details: The roads are the same. The walls are the same. I asked a clerk and he gave me a speech about 'administrative costs.'
My neighbor says it's all going into somebody's pocket. I don't know about that, but I can't say he's wrong.
reactions: I just pay and move on. Getting angry about it doesn't put coins back in my purse.

statCheck.pass: Administrative costs do go up when a settlement grows: more people means more ledgers. But not this much, not this fast.
statCheck.fail: I don't understand how these things work. I just know my share keeps getting bigger.

entries.intro: Say what you will about the folks running things around here: at least they fixed the well last month.
details: It took them long enough, and I'm sure someone's cousin got the contract, but it works now.
Clean water's clean water. I'll take it even if the process was a mess.
reactions: Sometimes things work out despite the people in charge, not because of them.
